Container.Name: owlet-capture
Container.Overview:
ONE-TIME, throwaway AuthKey capture for the native Owlet bridge. Bundles mitmproxy (web UI) + adb + frida-tools + the capture scripts. Deploy ALONGSIDE owlet-redroid (same owlet-net network). On start it auto-configures redroid (installs the mitmproxy CA, sets the proxy, starts frida-server), then prints the runbook in its log.
Set MITM_IP to your Unraid server IP so redroid's Android can reach the proxy.
USE: open the web UI (http://[IP]:8471, password 'owlet'). Install + drive the Owlet app via adb/scrcpy from your PC (adb connect [IP]:5577). Then in this container's Console run: /app/mitm/frida/run-frida.sh owlet-redroid:5555 unpin — log into the app, open the camera live view, and watch /captures for the UID + AuthKey. Remove this container + owlet-redroid when done.
Heads-up: the newest Owlet Dream app may refuse to log in on an emulator (Play Integrity) — see the project capture README for the GApps / phone fallback.

Config.Name: Web UI (host 8471)
Config.Description: mitmproxy web UI (password: owlet). Uncommon host port to avoid conflicts.

Config.Name: Proxy (host 8470)
Config.Description: HTTP proxy redroid's Android points at (MITM_IP:8470). Must match MITM_PORT.

Config.Name: Captures
Config.Description: Where the UID/AuthKey candidates + full flows are written.

Config.Name: mitm CA store
Config.Description: Persists the mitmproxy CA (installed into redroid).

Config.Name: MITM_IP
Config.Description: Your Unraid server IP — redroid's Android proxy points here. REQUIRED.

Config.Name: MITM_PORT
Config.Description: Proxy port — MUST match the Proxy port mapping above.

Config.Name: MITM_WEB_PASSWORD
Config.Description: Password for the mitmproxy web UI.
